scenes.voiceover: You never learned about money in school.
Your parents didn't talk about it helpfully.
So you became an adult with no idea how credit, compound interest, or budgeting works.
You avoid learning because it feels overwhelming.
In America, financial education isn't prioritized.
Schools don't teach it.
So millions are financially illiterate, navigating a complex system without a map.
This keeps you stuck.
You don't know how to get out of debt or how to invest.
You make decisions based on guesses, and often those decisions are wrong.
You pay overdraft fees.
Take out payday loans.
Don't contribute to a 401k.
Ignoring financial education means staying in the dark when information is available.
You could read a book, watch a video, take a free course.
But you don't.
Maybe you feel stupid for not knowing.
Maybe it feels boring.
But the less you know, the more mistakes you make, and those mistakes keep you broke.
Learning even basics gives you power.
Understanding how money works, how debt grows, and how savings compound will change your life.
The information is out there.
You just have to decide to learn.
